company_profile.name: AIM ImmunoTech Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: AIM ImmunoTech Inc., an immuno-pharma company, engages in the research and development of therapeutics to treat multiple types of cancers, viral diseases, and immune-deficiency disorders in the United States. The company's flagship products are Ampligen (rintatolimod), a drug of large macromolecular double-stranded ribonucleic acid molecules to treat chronic fatigue syndrome; and Alferon N Injection (Interferon alfa), a purified, natural-source, glycosylated, multi-species alpha interferon product for the treatment of genital warts. It is also developing Ampligen for potential treatment of pancreatic, renal cell carcinoma, malignant melanoma, non-small cell lung, ovarian, breast, colorectal, and prostate cancers, as well as for myalgic encephalomyelitis, Hepatitis B, HIV, COVID-19, and post-COVID conditions. The company was formerly known as Hemispherx Biopharma, Inc. and changed its name to AIM ImmunoTech Inc. in August 2019. AIM ImmunoTech Inc. was founded in 1966 and is headquartered in Ocala, Florida.
company_profile.description_summary: AIM ImmunoTech develops immunotherapeutics for cancers, viral diseases, and immune disorders. Its approved products include Ampligen for chronic fatigue syndrome and Alferon N Injection for genital warts, generating primary revenue. The company is actively researching Ampligen's potential across multiple cancer types and conditions including hepatitis B, HIV, and COVID-19.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

quality.note:
Moat score is null — AI Studio adds moat (0-10) and recalculates.
With moat: final_pct = (profitability + financial_strength + growth + predictability + moat) / 50 × 100.
Then re-classify using tiebreaker_inputs:
  ≥70%      → Safe (no penalty)
  65–69%    → Safe if 2+ of (roe≥0.13, roic≥0.13, eps_next_5y≥0.10) else Speculative (−3pts)
  55–64%    → Speculative (−3pts)
  50–54%    → Speculative if 2+ of (roe≥0.08, roic≥0.08, eps_next_5y≥0.08) else Dangerous (−8pts)
  <50%      → Dangerous (−8pts)
Moat override: if moat≥7 AND (profitability≥7 OR fs≥7) AND pct≥55 → Safe.
final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.name: Abundia Global Impact Group Inc
company_profile.type_rationale: Regulated utility — P/E and EV/EBITDA prioritised; high debt is structural
company_profile.description: Abundia Global Impact Group Inc., technology solutions company, focuses on converting waste into renewable fuels and chemicals in the United States. The company offers renewable diesel, including ultra low sulfur diesel and low carbon marine fuels; sustainable aviation fuel; and renewable naphtha and other chemical feedstocks. It operates in the recycling and renewable energy, environmental change, fuels, and chemicals sectors. The company is based in Houston, Texas. Abundia Global Impact Group Inc. is a subsidiary of Abundia Financial, Llc.
company_profile.description_summary:
This technology solutions company converts waste into renewable fuels and chemicals across the United States. It produces renewable diesel, sustainable aviation fuel, and renewable chemical feedstocks, generating revenue from the recycling, renewable energy, fuels, and chemicals sectors.

The company operates as a subsidiary of Abundia Financial, focusing on environmental impact through waste-to-fuel conversion technology.

_meta.type_rationale: Regulated utility — P/E and EV/EBITDA prioritised; high debt is structural
_meta.method_explanation: Utilities like electricity and water companies operate under government regulation, which means their prices and profits are capped but also very stable. They tend to carry a lot of debt because building infrastructure is expensive — but this is normal and expected for the industry. Because earnings are so predictable, Price-to-Earnings (P/E) and EV/EBITDA work well. DCF is less useful here because regulated utilities have limited ability to grow beyond what regulators allow.

quality.company_type_note: Banks and REITs carry structural leverage — do not describe financial_strength scores of 7+ as 'moderate concern' for these types.

company_profile.name: Ambow Education Holding Ltd. Am
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Ambow Education Holding Ltd. operates as an AI-driven technology educational company in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Educational programs and services, and HybriU licensing and sales. The company develops and offers HybriU, an AI driven platform, which is designed for the education, corporate conferencing, and live events industries to deliver immersive, intelligent, and real-time experiences, as well as engagement across in-person and remote audiences. It also provides career-focused post-secondary educational services to undergraduate students. Ambow Education Holding Ltd. was founded in 2000 and is headquartered in Cupertino, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company operates as an AI-driven educational technology provider serving both academic and corporate markets. It generates revenue through two main segments: educational programs and services for undergraduate students, and HybriU licensing and sales, an AI platform enabling immersive learning and conferencing experiences.

HybriU distinguishes the company by delivering intelligent, real-time engagement across in-person and remote audiences for education, corporate conferencing, and live events sectors.

company_profile.name: Amaze Holdings, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Amaze Holdings, Inc. engages in end-to-end, creator-powered commerce platform enables creators, brands, and consumers. It operates through e-commerce and subscriptions; and wine products segment. The company's platform streamlines product sales, subscription offerings, and digital content delivery. Its tools also support a diverse range of creators from independent digital entrepreneurs to small businesses by integrating storefront customization, payment processing, merchandising, and performance analytics. In addition, the company sells wines across the United States and Puerto Rico through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels to mass markets. Further, it offers tools for seamless product creation, advanced e-commerce solutions, and scalable managed services. The company was formerly known as Fresh Vine Wine, Inc. and changed its name to Amaze Holdings, Inc. in March 2025. Amaze Holdings, Inc. was founded in 2019 and is based in Costa Mesa, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
This creator-powered commerce platform enables creators, brands, and consumers to buy and sell products through integrated storefronts with payment processing, merchandising, and analytics tools. Revenue comes from e-commerce and subscriptions alongside a wine products segment sold through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels across the United States and Puerto Rico.

The platform distinguishes itself by serving diverse creators from independent digital entrepreneurs to small businesses, offering customizable storefronts, advanced e-commerce solutions, and scalable managed services alongside digital content delivery capabilities.

bull_points: <cite index="4-7">Amaze raised $11.7 million to eliminate debt and resolve accounts payable, aiming to be debt-free by Q1 2026</cite>, reducing near-term refinancing risk and improving cash flexibility for operations.

<cite index="2-3">Amaze expects to achieve near-profitability in Q4 2025 and GAAP profitability in Q1 2026, reflecting the seasonal strength in sales and operational improvements</cite>, marking a critical inflection point for investor sentiment.

<cite index="8-7">Amaze launched a Creator Commerce Media Platform on March 26, 2026 to monetize first-party transaction and audience data into media and data products</cite>, opening a new revenue stream ahead of Q2 2026 earnings.
bear_points: <cite index="8-1,8-2">2025 revenues were only $2.0 million with GMV $9.4 million, and the company recorded a $54.4 million operating loss, including a $34.3 million goodwill impairment</cite>, demonstrating severe profitability challenges despite growth projections.
<cite index="5-4">Amaze expects to enter 2026 with ~$3.5M cash</cite>, leaving minimal runway to absorb operational setbacks or delays in achieving profitability targets in Q1 2026.

The stock trades at $0.1876 versus an intrinsic value of $0.37, but profitability guidance is contingent on achieving aggressive 2026 revenue targets; any miss on Q1 2026 GAAP profitability could trigger sharp re-rating downward.

bull_points: <cite index="4-1,4-12">Amaze anticipates substantial growth in 2026, with projected net revenue for its commerce business at $7.3 million, reflecting a 52% annual growth rate, and the acquisition of The Food Channel is expected to contribute at least $6 million in topline revenue</cite>, providing a clear path to $13M+ combined revenue.

<cite index="5-5">2026 guidance includes $18.3M GMV and $7.3M net revenue for Amaze commerce</cite> while company operates in the creator economy with 2 billion unique visitors, positioning it to scale with secular tailwinds in creator-powered commerce.

With <cite index="5-4">management expecting the company to be debt-free by Q1 2026</cite>, the balance sheet inflection removes a structural overhang and improves return on equity from deeply negative (-143.61%) as deleveraging compounds with revenue growth.
bear_points: Amaze's financial strength score of only 1/10 and current ratio of 0.18 reflect a severely undercapitalized balance sheet; <cite index="6-7">net profit for the twelve months ending Sep 30, 2025 was -$12.40 million</cite>, showing the company is still hemorrhaging cash despite growth rhetoric.

<cite index="8-1,8-2">2025 revenues of only $2.0 million with a $34.3 million goodwill impairment</cite> suggests prior acquisitions have destroyed value; the company must prove it can execute on Food Channel integration and AI platform monetization without further writedowns.

The 679% revenue growth metric is misleading given the company's minuscule base; with negative 2475.4% profit margins and zero 5-year EPS growth projection, achieving sustainable profitability and positive returns depends on flawless execution in a competitive creator commerce market where larger players continue to gain share.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or significantly overvalued — better opportunities elsewhere.

company_profile.name: Armata Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Armata Pharmaceuticals, Inc. operates as a clinical-stage biotechnology company that focuses on the development of targeted bacteriophage therapeutics for antibiotic-resistant infections worldwide. It develops its products using its proprietary bacteriophage-based technology. The company's product candidates include AP-SA02 for the treatment of Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia; AP-PA03 for Pseudomonas aeruginosa . The company is based in Los Angeles, California. Armata Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a subsidiary of Innoviva, Inc.
company_profile.description_summary:
A clinical-stage biotechnology company developing targeted bacteriophage therapeutics to treat antibiotic-resistant bacterial infections. Armata uses proprietary bacteriophage technology to create product candidates, including AP-SA02 for Staphylococcus aureus infections and AP-PA03 for Pseudomonas aeruginosa treatment. The company currently generates no significant revenue as it advances clinical candidates.

The company distinguishes itself through its focus on bacteriophage-based treatments, which target drug-resistant pathogens using naturally occurring viruses rather than traditional antibiotics. As a subsidiary of Innoviva, it operates from Los Angeles, California.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (14.0%/yr): growth in line with analyst expectations. Bull (18.3%/yr): everything goes to plan. Bear (7.7%/yr): growth disappoints. Growth capped at 14.0% (from 40.1%) — the balance sheet (financial strength 1/10) limits how fast this company can realistically grow.

company_profile.name: AtlasClear Holdings, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
company_profile.description: AtlasClear Holdings, Inc. operates as a technology enabled financial services firm. It creates a platform for trading, clearing, settlement, and banking of financial products, such as crypto for financial services firms. The company offers fintech assets, including AtlasFX and Rubicon, an order management system and trading application for the automated management of currency exchange; SURFACExchange, a multi-lateral trading platform for over the counter spot and options FX trading; and BondQuantum, a real time advance analytical program for the analysis of bonds. It also engages in the provision of back and middle office solutions; and securities broker and dealer, as well as dealing in over-the-counter and listed securities. AtlasClear Holdings, Inc. is based in Tampa, Florida.
company_profile.description_summary:
This technology-enabled financial services firm operates a platform for trading, clearing, settlement, and banking of financial products, particularly cryptocurrency. Revenue derives from its suite of fintech solutions, including trading applications, multi-lateral trading platforms, and analytical software, alongside broker-dealer services and back-office solutions.

The company distinguishes itself through specialized products like AtlasFX for currency exchange management, SURFACExchange for over-the-counter FX trading, and BondQuantum for real-time bond analysis, serving institutional financial services clients.

_meta.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
_meta.method_explanation: Asset managers and brokers earn fees for managing other people's money. Their balance sheets hold client assets that do not belong to the firm, which makes standard balance sheet metrics like Price-to-Book misleading. Instead, we focus on net income and revenue-based methods, which better reflect the underlying fee-earning power of the business.

quality.company_type_note: Banks and REITs carry structural leverage — do not describe financial_strength scores of 7+ as 'moderate concern' for these types.

company_profile.name: Austin Gold Corp.
company_profile.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
company_profile.description: Austin Gold Corp., a gold exploration company, focuses on the acquisition, exploration, and evaluation of mineral resource properties in Nevada. It holds 70% interest in the Kelly Creek Project, which covers an area of 21.4 square kilometers located in the Kelly Creek Basin, Humboldt County, Nevada; and 100% interest in the Stockade Mountain Project comprising 261 unpatented lode mining claims, which covers an area of 2748 hectares located in Malheur County, Oregon. It also holds interest in the Lone Mountain Project consisting of 802 unpatented lode mining and patented mining claims, which covers an area of 57.6 square kilometers located in Elko County, Nevada. The company was incorporated in 2020 and is based in Vancouver, Canada.
company_profile.description_summary: Focuses on acquiring, exploring, and evaluating mineral resource properties in Nevada and Oregon. Holds majority or full interests in three gold exploration projects: Kelly Creek in Nevada, Stockade Mountain in Oregon, and Lone Mountain in Nevada. Revenue derives from mineral exploration activities and property development.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

company_profile.name: AXIL Brands, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: AXIL Brands, Inc. engages in the manufacture, marketing, distribution, and sale of hearing and audio enhancement and protection products in the United States, Canada, Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Africa, and Asia. The company operates through Hearing Enhancement and Protection, and Hair and Skin Care segments. It provides ear plugs, earmuffs, ear buds, and outdoor speakers under the AXIL brand. The company also provides shampoo, conditioner, scalp treatments, styling aids, and skin health solutions under the Reviv3 Procare brand, as well as cleansing, conditioning, repair, protection, and volume enhancement products. It sells GS Extreme, a Bluetooth-enabled earbuds for sound enhancement and hearing protection; XCOR True Wireless, a Digital earbud with touch control; TRACKR Blu, a Bluetooth audio earmuffs with advanced sound enhancement and protection; and X-PRO, a passive ear protections. The company sells its products through direct-to-consumer e-commerce channels, various third-party online platforms, distributors, and salon partnerships. It serves the sporting goods, recreational, military, federal agent, law enforcement, tactical, and industrial sectors. The company was formerly known as Reviv3 Procare Company and changed its name to AXIL Brands, Inc. in February 2024. AXIL Brands, Inc. was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in Beverly Hills, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company manufactures and sells hearing enhancement and protection products including earplugs, earmuffs, and Bluetooth-enabled earbuds under the AXIL brand, generating primary revenue from this segment. It also produces hair and skin care products under the Reviv3 Procare brand. The company distributes globally through e-commerce, online platforms, distributors, and salon partnerships, serving sporting goods, recreational, military, and industrial sectors.

The company distinguishes itself by combining audio technology with hearing protection, offering advanced features like Bluetooth connectivity and digital touch controls in its products, while maintaining a diversified portfolio through its complementary beauty care segment.
